title: I've Got An Arrow Here;
content:
I've got an arrow here;
    Loving the hand that sent it,
    I the dart revere.

    Fell, they will say, in 'skirmish'!
    Vanquished, my soul will know,
    By but a simple arrow
    Sped by an archer's bow.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "I've Got An Arrow Here;", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
